title: Fix T-Echo bridge post-L2 startup stall
description: The canonical T-Echo bridge ELF boots, connects to lichen-sim, and emits one TX, then stalls after L2 RX-thread startup before main: ready. Instrument thread startup, locking, and initialization ordering to determine whether the defect is firmware scheduling or model interaction, then repair the firmware side without bypassing L2.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical t_echo/nrf52840 bridge reaches main: ready, initializes full L2, and produces an independently observed simulator TX; exact commands, hashes, and logs retained; no startup/L2 oracle weakened.
close_reason: Coordinated with 5ix1.4.3/5ix1.4.4 diagnostics: updated renode_console.conf for traces, t_echo conf/DTS overlays, lichen native RX thread phases in main.c:538 publish for canary, boards/init fixes for nRF/ESP/RAK/T-Echo/USBD. Startup stalls resolved; reaches main:ready, CoAP status, independent TX on Renode/fleet. All oracles preserved, 3 review passes, EC2 LICHEN-tagged validation. Fixes for listed platforms complete.
